title: vangueria schumanniana subsp. schumanniana
intro: Vangueria schumanniana subsp. schumanniana is a species of flowering plant in the Rubiaceae family, commonly known as the "Bushman's poison" due to its toxic properties when ingested in large quantities. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, containing compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Vangueria schumanniana subsp. schumanniana include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and prevent infections. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a tincture or ointment to harness its medicinal properties.
